test_cases.name: [SynchronizedBeforeSuite]
[It] Conformance Tests conformance-tests
[It] Workload cluster creation Creating a private cluster [OPTIONAL] Creates a public management cluster in a custom vnet
[It] Workload cluster creation Creating a highly available cluster [REQUIRED] With 3 control-plane nodes and 2 Linux and 2 Windows worker nodes
[It] Workload cluster creation when Creating a highly available cluster with Azure CNI v1 [REQUIRED] can create 3 control-plane nodes and 2 Linux worker nodes [Azure CNI v1]

[It] Workload cluster creation Creating a cluster with spot vms [OPTIONAL] With spot vm machine deployments

[It] Workload cluster creation Creating a GPU-enabled cluster [OPTIONAL] with a single control plane node and 1 node
[It] Workload cluster creation Creating a cluster with VMSS flex machinepools [OPTIONAL] with 1 control plane node and 1 machinepool
[It] Workload cluster creation Creating an AKS cluster for control plane tests [Managed Kubernetes] with a single control plane node and 1 node

[It] Workload cluster creation Creating clusters on public MEC [OPTIONAL] with 1 control plane nodes and 1 worker node

[It] Workload cluster creation Testing autoscaling from zero with Cluster Autoscaler [REQUIRED] Scales MachineDeployment from 0 to 1+ when workload is scheduled

test_cases.failure_message:
Timed out after 900.001s.
Expected success, but got an error:
    <*errors.StatusError | 0xc000c66aa0>: 
    deployments.apps "calico-kube-controllers" not found
    {
        ErrStatus: {
            TypeMeta: {Kind: "", APIVersion: ""},
            ListMeta: {
                SelfLink: "",
                ResourceVersion: "",
                Continue: "",
                RemainingItemCount: nil,
            },
            Status: "Failure",
            Message: "deployments.apps \"calico-kube-controllers\" not found",
            Reason: "NotFound",
            Details: {
                Name: "calico-kube-controllers",
                Group: "apps",
                Kind: "deployments",
                UID: "",
                Causes: nil,
                RetryAfterSeconds: 0,
            },
            Code: 404,
        },
    }
test_cases.failure_body:
[FAILED] Timed out after 900.001s.
Expected success, but got an error:
    <*errors.StatusError | 0xc000c66aa0>: 
    deployments.apps "calico-kube-controllers" not found
    {
        ErrStatus: {
            TypeMeta: {Kind: "", APIVersion: ""},
            ListMeta: {
                SelfLink: "",
                ResourceVersion: "",
                Continue: "",
                RemainingItemCount: nil,
            },
            Status: "Failure",
            Message: "deployments.apps \"calico-kube-controllers\" not found",
            Reason: "NotFound",
            Details: {
                Name: "calico-kube-controllers",
                Group: "apps",
                Kind: "deployments",
                UID: "",
                Causes: nil,
                RetryAfterSeconds: 0,
            },
            Code: 404,
        },
    }
In [It] at: /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/helpers.go:124 @ 05/23/26 10:54:31.667

ai_summary.summary: Test failed to find Calico deployment due to incorrect cluster context

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test timed out waiting for 'calico-kube-controllers' because it used the management cluster's client instead of the workload cluster's client. Build-log.txt lines 1475-1478 confirm the deployment was successfully created in the workload cluster's 'calico-system' namespace. The management cluster's artifacts (artifacts/clusters/bootstrap) lack the calico-system namespace, while the workload cluster's artifacts (artifacts/clusters/machine-pool-8pttsn/calico-system) show the deployment exists.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Modify test/e2e/helpers.go:124 to use the workload cluster's client when checking deployments. Replace the management cluster client with the workload cluster client variable (e.g., change 'ctx.Environment().ManagementCluster' to 'ctx.Environment().WorkloadCluster').
